title: AI is changing how small online sellers decide what to make
optimized_headline: How AI is Transforming Product Decisions for Small Online Sellers
raw_excerpt: For years Mike McClary sold the Guardian LTE Flashlight, a heavy-duty black model, online through his small outdoor brand. The product, designed for brightness and durability, became one of his most popular items ever. Even after he stopped offering it around 2017, customers kept sending him emails asking where they could buy it.  When McClary…
raw_body: For years Mike McClary sold the Guardian LTE Flashlight, a heavy-duty black model, online through his small outdoor brand. The product, designed for brightness and durability, became one of his most popular items ever. Even after he stopped offering it around 2017, customers kept sending him emails asking where they could buy it.  When McClary…
speedrun: AI is transforming how small online sellers like Mike McClary decide what products to offer. After discontinuing his popular Guardian LTE Flashlight in 2017, McClary continued to receive customer inquiries about it. Now, AI tools help sellers analyze past demand and predict future trends, enabling them to make informed decisions about product offerings. This shift matters because it allows small businesses to better meet customer needs and optimize inventory.
why_it_matters: Small sellers can leverage AI to respond to customer demand more effectively, improving sales and satisfaction.
The rise of AI in retail indicates a broader trend where data-driven insights are becoming essential for competitive advantage.

lenses.eli12: AI is helping small online sellers figure out what to sell by analyzing customer demand. It's like having a smart assistant that tells you what people want based on past purchases. This matters because it can help everyday sellers choose products that customers are eager to buy, boosting their chances of success.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, AI tools offer insights into customer preferences, making it easier to decide which products to develop or stock. This can reduce costs by minimizing unsold inventory and improve efficiency in product launches. Understanding customer demand through AI could lead to more successful product offerings.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, AI models analyze historical sales data to identify trends and predict future demand. These models can incorporate variables such as seasonality and customer feedback to enhance accuracy. However, sellers should be mindful of the data quality they feed into these systems to ensure reliable outputs.
